source_name: Australian oil and gas production by state/jurisdiction

fields.as_of: Most recent full fiscal year reported in the Resources and Energy Quarterly (RE-Q) and DCCEEW Australian Petroleum Statistics at the time of keying.
fields.primary_source_document: Department of Industry, Science and Resources — Resources and Energy Quarterly; DCCEEW — Australian Petroleum Statistics (APS).

states.name: Western Australia
states.headline_framing: Dominant share of national gas and LNG production, anchored by the North West Shelf, Pluto, Gorgon, Wheatstone and Prelude projects. Major offshore fields sit in Commonwealth waters adjacent to WA; the North West Shelf royalty is shared between the Commonwealth and WA under the 1979 Heads of Agreement.
states.royalty_regime: Commonwealth/WA royalties on North West Shelf (pre-PRRT grandfathered); PRRT on later offshore projects. State royalties on onshore gas.
states.export_orientation: Overwhelmingly export: majority of Karratha and Pilbara LNG flows to North Asia.
states.share_pct_note: Key the most recent RE-Q state share here when refreshing.

states.name: Queensland
states.headline_framing: The only Australian LNG stream produced from coal seam gas (CSG). Three Gladstone-based trains — QCLNG, APLNG, GLNG — ship to Asia. QLD gas supplies the east-coast domestic market alongside exports.
states.royalty_regime: State petroleum royalty under QLD Petroleum and Gas (Production and Safety) Act. Transitioned to a value-based royalty with tiered price bands from 2023.
states.export_orientation: Mixed: LNG export plus east-coast domestic supply.
states.share_pct_note: Key the most recent RE-Q state share here when refreshing.

states.name: Northern Territory
states.headline_framing: Ichthys LNG (operated by INPEX) is the anchor LNG stream; gas is piped from the Browse Basin offshore WA to Darwin for processing. Beetaloo Basin is the onshore frontier under active exploration.
states.royalty_regime: PRRT on offshore Ichthys production; NT territory royalties on onshore gas.
states.share_pct_note: Key the most recent RE-Q state share here when refreshing.

states.name: South Australia
states.headline_framing: Cooper Basin (Moomba) is the historical onshore producer; gas supplies the east-coast market via the Moomba-Sydney pipeline.
states.royalty_regime: State petroleum royalty under the Petroleum and Geothermal Energy Act.
states.export_orientation: Primarily domestic east-coast supply; some liquids export via ports.
states.share_pct_note: Key the most recent RE-Q state share here when refreshing.

states.name: Victoria
states.headline_framing: Bass Strait (Gippsland Basin, ExxonMobil/Esso-BHP JV) is the long-standing east-coast gas source; output is in structural decline as legacy fields deplete.
states.royalty_regime: Commonwealth offshore Bass Strait arrangements predate PRRT; state royalty on Victorian onshore gas.
states.share_pct_note: Key the most recent RE-Q state share here when refreshing.

fields.percent_share_population_note: Numerical state-share percentages are deliberately left null in this first pass because the Resources and Energy Quarterly presents production by commodity and quarter rather than a single consolidated state share for oil-plus-gas. Keying requires choosing a commodity basis (e.g. petajoule production of natural gas, or LNG tonnes shipped) and citing it explicitly. Do not fabricate a combined share.

notes: State-by-state framing of Australian oil and gas production. WA dominates gas/LNG; QLD is the sole CSG-to-LNG producer; NT is offshore-Ichthys-anchored; SA is Cooper Basin gas to the east coast; VIC is the declining Bass Strait. Numerical shares are deliberately left null until a documented commodity basis can be keyed from the most recent Resources and Energy Quarterly.
citation: Department of Industry, Science and Resources, Resources and Energy Quarterly (industry.gov.au); Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water, Australian Petroleum Statistics (energy.gov.au).
